ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clothing_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the clothing category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing size */,
  "size_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing size */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the clothing size */,
  "size_chart" TEXT /* Size chart for the clothing size */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clothing category the product belongs to */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the clothing product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the clothing product */,
  "stock_level" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the clothing product */,
  "style" TEXT /* Style of the clothing product */,
  "season" TEXT /* Season for which the clothing product is suitable */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the clothing product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES clothing_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_sizes" (
  "product_size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product size */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the size belongs to */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size */,
  "chest_width" TEXT /* Chest width measurement for the product size */,
  "waist_width" TEXT /* Waist width measurement for the product size */,
  "hips_width" TEXT /* Hips width measurement for the product size */,
  "height" TEXT /* Height measurement for the product size */,
  "inseam" TEXT /* Inseam measurement for the product size */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES sizes ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fabric_types" (
  "fabric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fabric type */,
  "fabric_name" TEXT /* Name of the fabric type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the fabric type */,
  "material_composition" TEXT /* Material composition of the fabric type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fabric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "colors" (
  "color_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each color */,
  "color_name" TEXT /* Name of the color */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the color */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("color_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_variants" (
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product variant */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the variant belongs to */,
  "fabric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fabric type used in the variant */,
  "color_id" INTEGER /* ID of the color used in the variant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_variants_fabric_id FOREIGN KEY ("fabric_id") REFERENCES fabric_types ("fabric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_variants_color_id FOREIGN KEY ("color_id") REFERENCES colors ("color_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_variants_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the inventory entry belongs to */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse the inventory entry belongs to */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in the warehouse */,
  "inventory_management_status" TEXT /* Inventory management status of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was made */,
  "total" REAL /* Total cost of the order */,
  "shipping_details" TEXT /* Shipping details for the order */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used by the customer */,
  "discount" REAL /* Discount applied to the order */,
  "sales_representative" TEXT /* Sales representative who made the sale */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order the item belongs to */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product the item belongs to */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "customer_segment" TEXT /* Customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, inventory manager, sales staff) */,
  "login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of login attempts made by the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
